findings.message: Comprehension quiz ready (14 questions, pass ≥ 80%). Complete it on the dashboard BEFORE Approve & Merge — shipping code you cannot explain is the risk.
findings.suggestion: Open the review dashboard → read the study guide → take the quiz.

findings.message: Manual dev task: After changing governance rules: run `cd governance && pytest` and `ai-guardrail run --root ..` before opening a PR.

study_guide.elevator_pitch: This change touches the Shadow AI Guardrail Gateway — an enterprise proxy that sits between users and public LLMs so sensitive data can be inspected pre-flight. Files in this change include: app/proxy/payloads.py, app/proxy/providers/anthropic.py, governance/governance/steps/fuzz_chamber.py, governance/governance/steps/benchmark_engine.py, governance/tests/test_fuzz_benchmark_helpers.py, governance/README.md.
study_guide.bigger_picture: Bigger picture: Phase 1 builds the async FastAPI proxy; Phase 2 adds PII scrubbing; Phase 3 adds Postgres audit logs; Phase 4 packages with Docker/Terraform. The governance suite (this quiz included) gates merges so you never ship AI-written code you cannot explain.

glossary.definition: Inspecting/normalizing an outbound LLM request BEFORE any bytes leave your network toward OpenAI/Anthropic. This is the choke point.
A service that sits between the user and the public AI API. Clients talk to YOUR gateway; the gateway talks to the AI provider.
A Python web framework for building HTTP APIs. Routes like POST /v1/chat/completions are defined here.
Python concurrency style that lets the server handle many requests while waiting on network I/O (e.g. calling OpenAI) without blocking.
A secret or config value injected at runtime (e.g. OPENAI_API_KEY). Never commit real keys into git.
The outside LLM service the gateway can call, such as OpenAI or Anthropic. Phase 1 chooses one per request or from config.
Code that translates our internal request shape into OpenAI's or Anthropic's specific API format.
The external provider API reached after the gateway route and human-owned interceptor checkpoint have allowed the request forward.
A response mode where tokens/chunks flow back over time instead of waiting for one complete JSON response.
Not Implemented. In Phase 1 it means Checkpoint #1 is still pending and the human must complete the interceptor before provider forwarding.

key_functions.name: to_anthropic_payload
key_functions.plain_english: Map OpenAI-compatible gateway payload to Anthropic Messages API.

key_functions.name: _explicit_fuzz_targets
key_functions.plain_english: A function named `_explicit_fuzz_targets` in `governance/governance/steps/fuzz_chamber.py`. Arguments: tree.

key_functions.name: _discover_fuzz_targets
key_functions.plain_english: Find top-level functions that look like pure helpers (1–2 args).

key_functions.name: _run_harness
key_functions.plain_english: A function named `_run_harness` in `governance/governance/steps/fuzz_chamber.py`. Arguments: path, func_name, timeout_s.

key_functions.name: run
key_functions.plain_english:
Deterministically fuzz discovered helper functions with boundary inputs.

Runs in a subprocess sandbox (Docker optional later). Crashes are ERRORS.
TypeError / ValueError contract rejections are informational only.

key_functions.name: _time_call
key_functions.plain_english: A function named `_time_call` in `governance/governance/steps/benchmark_engine.py`. Arguments: fn, n, repeats.

key_functions.name: estimate_big_o
key_functions.plain_english:
Derive a rough Big-O label from size/time pairs using log-log slope.

slope ≈ 0 → O(1)
slope ≈ 1 → O(N)
slope ≈ 2 → O(N²)

key_functions.name: _linear_scan
key_functions.plain_english: A function named `_linear_scan` in `governance/governance/steps/benchmark_engine.py`. Arguments: data.

key_functions.name: _quadratic_scan
key_functions.plain_english: A function named `_quadratic_scan` in `governance/governance/steps/benchmark_engine.py`. Arguments: data.

key_functions.name: _hash_join
key_functions.plain_english: A function named `_hash_join` in `governance/governance/steps/benchmark_engine.py`. Arguments: data.

key_functions.name: profile_target
key_functions.plain_english: Profile a caller-supplied target function over its configured sizes.

key_functions.name: profile_targets
key_functions.plain_english: Profile all caller-supplied targets by name.

manual_dev_tasks: After changing governance rules: run `cd governance && pytest` and `ai-guardrail run --root ..` before opening a PR.
security_notes: Never commit real API keys — use environment variables.
The interceptor is the security choke point: bad validation here means prompts can leave the network unchecked later.
Passing Bugbot/Vercel alone is not enough — AST/OWASP/fuzz/copyright + this comprehension quiz must clear before merge.

questions.prompt: What does **pre-flight** mean in this project?
choices: Running unit tests after deploying to production
Inspecting/normalizing an LLM request BEFORE it leaves your network
Formatting Python code with Black
Buying a plane ticket for an on-call engineer
questions.explanation: Pre-flight is the choke point: validate/scrub before any upstream provider call.

choices: A React weather app
A CDN that caches images
A proxy between users and public LLM APIs that can inspect outbound prompts
A database of LeetCode solutions
questions.explanation: It is an enterprise security proxy for outbound LLM traffic.

choices: Skip scrubbing and go straight to Terraform on day one
Phase 1 proxy → Phase 2 scrubbing → Phase 3 Postgres audit → Phase 4 Docker/Terraform
Only build a Next.js marketing site
Host the streaming proxy on Vercel serverless
questions.explanation: Crawl → Walk → Run → Cloud. Vercel is forbidden for the streaming proxy.

questions.prompt: Why does this comprehension quiz exist before merge?
choices: GitHub requires emojis on every PR
So you can rubber-stamp AI code without reading it
Because merging code you cannot explain is dangerous — you must understand it first
To replace writing tests forever
questions.explanation: You are learning; AI wrote a lot of this. Understanding is the resume-defense gate.

choices: A async function defined in this codebase that you should be able to explain
A built-in Python keyword like `if`
An AWS region name
A CSS class in the dashboard
questions.explanation: `to_anthropic_payload` is application code in `app/proxy/payloads.py`. Args: payload.

questions.prompt: Roughly, what is the happy-path request flow for the gateway?
choices: Browser → OpenAI directly (gateway unused)
Client → Gateway route → interceptor → provider adapter → upstream LLM → stream back
Client → Postgres → Terraform → done
Client → Bugbot → Vercel → merge
questions.explanation: The gateway owns the path; providers are adapters behind the interceptor.

choices: The outside LLM service selected for a request, such as OpenAI or Anthropic
A CSS theme provider in the dashboard
The person approving the PR
A local pytest fixture only
questions.explanation: The provider is the external LLM service; the gateway chooses it from the request or default config.

questions.prompt: When should the chat route choose and call a provider adapter?
choices: Before parsing the request body
Only after `intercept_outbound_request` succeeds
Inside the copyright filter
After returning the response to the client
questions.explanation: Checkpoint #1 is the pre-flight gate. Provider forwarding happens only after it succeeds.

questions.prompt: What is different when `stream=True` in the Phase 1 gateway?
choices: The gateway buffers the whole answer and returns one JSON object
The gateway asks the provider for a stream and relays chunks/SSE back to the client
The request skips the interceptor because streaming is faster
The provider is always ignored
questions.explanation: Streaming still goes through the checkpoint; only the response delivery changes to chunk/SSE relay.

questions.prompt: What does HTTP 501 mean for Checkpoint #1 in this project?
choices: The provider API key is definitely wrong
The human-owned interceptor is still pending; agents must not fill `app/proxy/interceptor.py`
Streaming responses are unsupported forever
The copyright filter found a LeetCode snippet
questions.explanation: Checkpoint #1 is human-owned: the human must complete `app/proxy/interceptor.py`; agents must not soften or bypass it.

questions.prompt: When we say **dependencies** for a change, what should you check?
choices: Only the color of the README badge
Libraries/modules this code imports or relies on (e.g. FastAPI, httpx) plus sibling project files it calls
Whether the moon is full
Only the number of emojis in the commit message
questions.explanation: Detected imports in this scan: __future__, app, ast, collections, dataclasses, governance, httpx, json, math, pathlib, subprocess, tempfile.

questions.prompt: If a file has `TODO: Human Hands-On Implementation` or `NotImplementedError`, what should you do?
choices: Ignore it and merge anyway
Ask an agent to silently fill it with no learning
Treat it as YOUR job — implement/understand it before claiming the resume bullet
Delete the whole repository
questions.explanation: The ledger forbids agents from auto-completing human checkpoints.

questions.prompt: Which is a security red flag in a PR?
choices: Using pydantic-settings to read env vars
A hardcoded `API_KEY = "sk-..."` string in source
Adding a `/health` endpoint
Writing a README
questions.explanation: Hardcoded secrets are critical findings in the OWASP auditor.

questions.prompt: Why is reviewing AI-generated code without understanding it dangerous?
choices: It isn't — AI is always correct
You might approve insecure, wrong, or unmaintainable logic you cannot defend in an interview or outage
GitHub will revoke your account for reading code
Tests become illegal
questions.explanation: This quiz exists so 'human review' means informed review, not a blind click.

summary.comprehension_note: Step 6 quiz must be passed on the dashboard before Step 7 merge.
